name: Bi-Level Chocolate Apricot Butter Tart
recipeIngredient: 3/4 cup Semi-sweet chocolate squares or chocolate chips, melted
recipeInstructions:
In a large bowl, combine the flour, sugar, softened butter, and melted chocolate. Add the ground almonds and mix well.
Press the mixture into the bottom of a 9-inch Springform Pan.
For the Butter Layer: In another bowl, cream together the butter and sugar. Add the egg yolks, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, lemon rind, and almond extract. Beat until smooth. Gradually add the flour and baking powder, mixing until well combined. Refrigerate for about 30 minutes.
Divide the dough into two portions. Chill one portion for 30 minutes to set it slightly. Press the chilled dough slightly smaller than the pan onto a lightly floured surface. Carefully transfer it on top of the chocolate crust layer, pressing it into the pan and up the sides by about half an inch.
After chilling the remaining dough, combine the apricot jam with 1 tablespoon lemon juice. Spread over the butter layer.
Roll out the remaining dough on a floured surface and cut into strips that are approximately 5/8 inch wide. Arrange the strips in a lattice pattern on top of the tart. Bake at 350°F for 40 to 43 minutes. Garnish with powdered sugar and mint leaves before serving.
